feature_control.fallback: omit desktop and qa from explicit selection; Apple and other selected routes remain unchanged
stop_conditions: coverage compiler input violates risk contract
quality evidence cannot be separated from verification evidence
desktop framework or module root is ambiguous
desktop adapter command or permission is not frozen

limitations: native macOS build and verification remain delegated to the selected Apple provider; Desktop never invokes xcodebuild directly
frameworks without a frozen adapter command remain blocked or manual rather than using shell inference

goals: platform-neutral QA artifacts and risk-based coverage
PRD, bug, and release workflow closure
replaceable Desktop provider and environment evidence
non_goals: embedding Desktop framework commands in QA Core
treating verification passed as QA passed
activating unselected platform or runtime packages

security_invariants: public QA workflow compilers are planning-only and reject outcome or evidence fields
QA aggregation accepts only validated caller-supplied Workflow Plan, RunLedger, TestResult, Defect, Regression, and evidence artifacts
current multi-platform validation status is the deterministic worst status across latest node attempts
unresolved defects and stale or blocked regression ownership cannot be hidden by release go or workflow passed
Desktop QA artifacts share the frozen Desktop environment fingerprint and stale on environment changes
Desktop adapter permissions that are denied or unknown fail closed

validation.minimum_command: PYTHONPATH=src:. python3 -m unittest tests.test_qa_workflows tests.test_qa_runtime tests.test_desktop_provider tests.test_qa_contracts tests.test_planning tests.test_runtime -v
